target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series A-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors that has raised $125M+ and carries a post-money valuation of $5.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private-stage peers, with primary focus on 3 direct-stage comparables: d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), and Rain AI ($500M Series A).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to Etched's $5.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A advancement, implies $10.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 24.2x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied; Etched's revenue is not disclosed, limiting reliance on this method for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $5.5B. Etched's current $5.0B post-money sits near the median, reflecting the tension between stage-progression upside and peer-median anchoring. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the durability of valuations in the early-stage semiconductor infrastructure sector.
